title: Arithmetic hyperbolic 3-manifolds, perfectoid spaces, and Galois representations III
speaker: Peter Scholze
abstract: One of the most studied objects in mathematics is the modular curve, which is the quotient of hyperbolic 2-space by the action of \(\mathrm{SL}_2(\mathbb Z)\). It is naturally the home of modular forms, but it also admits an algebraic structure. The interplay of these structures lies at the heart of the Langlands correspondence for \(\mathrm{GL}_2/\mathbb Q\), connecting modular forms with Galois representations. The natural generalizations of the modular curve to higher dimension are the arithmetic locally symmetric spaces.

proof_stages.label: Target mod-p Galois representation theorem
proof_stages.summary: The talk states the goal: from any mod-p Hecke eigensystem in the homology of a locally symmetric space for GL_n over a totally real or CM field, construct a Galois representation with Frobenius characteristic polynomials governed by the Hecke eigenvalues, then specialize the discussion to GL_g over Q.

proof_stages.label: Known characteristic-zero input from Siegel cusp forms
proof_stages.summary: Siegel modular varieties A_g and holomorphic cuspidal Siegel eigenforms are recalled, together with the known construction of associated (2g+1)-dimensional p-adic Galois representations via endoscopic transfer from symplectic groups to self-dual automorphic representations of GL_{2g+1} and the Harris–Taylor/Shin-type Galois representation theorems.

proof_stages.label: Borel-Serre boundary realizes GL_g homology inside Siegel homology
proof_stages.summary: The Borel-Serre compactification of the Siegel variety has boundary strata indexed by rational parabolics, and the stratum for the parabolic with Levi GL_g gives Eisenstein boundary contributions through which torsion homology of the GL_g locally symmetric space appears in the mod-p homology of A_g.

proof_stages.label: Lifting torsion Siegel Hecke eigensystems to cusp forms
proof_stages.summary: The key technical theorem asserts that any mod-p Hecke eigensystem occurring in torsion homology of a Siegel/Shimura variety is congruent modulo p to the Hecke eigensystem of a holomorphic cuspidal eigenform, possibly after changing p-level and weight.

proof_stages.label: Deduction and extraction of the desired GL_g representation
proof_stages.summary: Starting from a GL_g torsion class, the boundary map moves it to Siegel torsion homology, the lifting theorem supplies a congruent classical Siegel cusp form, the known characteristic-zero theory supplies a (2g+1)-dimensional Galois representation, and its mod-p reduction is shown to decompose as the desired g-dimensional representation together with its dual and a trivial summand.

proof_stages.label: p-adic Hodge-theoretic replacement for the classical period picture
proof_stages.summary: Because the lifting theorem is a congruence modulo p, the proof must work p-adically: the classical Hodge-de Rham filtration is not accessible from the p-adic Tate module over Cp, so the Hodge-Tate spectral sequence is introduced to put the needed filtration directly on p-adic étale cohomology.

proof_stages.label: Perfectoid infinite p-level tower and Hodge-Tate period map
proof_stages.summary: Passing up the p-power level tower of modular/Siegel varieties in the category of adic spaces produces a perfectoid infinite-level space, and the Hodge-Tate filtration defines a period map to projective space that is GL_2(Q_p)-equivariant and kills prime-to-p Hecke orbits.

proof_stages.label: Stratified geometry of the Hodge-Tate period map
proof_stages.summary: The Hodge-Tate period map is described on ordinary and supersingular strata: the ordinary locus maps according to the canonical subgroup, while the supersingular locus is governed by the Lubin-Tate tower and its Faltings duality with the Drinfeld tower, yielding highly non-finite-type fiber behavior.

examples.name: Compact Shimura-variety case of the lifting theorem
examples.note: The compact case is used to illustrate that the key theorem can be viewed simply as saying that torsion Hecke classes lift, in a weak congruence sense, to characteristic zero.
examples.engagement_reason: the compact Shimura-variety case is only described as a special case where the theorem says torsion lifts to characteristic zero
